#libs
```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(phyloseq)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(ggpubr)  # for stat_compare_means
library(tidyr)   # for drop_na if needed

```



#load ps
```{r}
ps1 <- readRDS('/home/sonec_iv/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_all_physeq_wgs_COUNTS_v2.rds')
ps1
ps1@sam_data$Batch <- as.factor(ps1@sam_data$Batch)
ps = prune_taxa(taxa_sums(ps1) > 10, ps1)
level <- 'Species'
ps <- speedyseq::tax_glom(ps, taxrank = level, NArm = TRUE)
det <- 1/100
prev <- 10/100
# Prepare the phyloseq object
taxas <- core_members(ps, detection = det, prevalence = prev, include.lowest = F)
ps <- prune_taxa(taxas, ps)

```




# debug
```{r}
# SIMPLE AND ROBUST SOLUTION - FIXED VERSION

calculate_within_sample_bray_curtis <- function(ps_obj) {
  cat("=== Calculating Within-Sample Bray-Curtis Distances ===\n")
  
  # Get metadata as dataframe
  meta_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj))
  
  # Get unique patients with both timepoints
  valid_patients <- meta_df %>%
    group_by(No_Patient) %>%
    summarise(
      has_both = all(c("-1", "-2") %in% Timepoint),
      Group = first(Group)
    ) %>%
    filter(has_both)
  
  cat("Found", nrow(valid_patients), "patients with both timepoints\n")
  
  if (nrow(valid_patients) == 0) {
    cat("No patients with both timepoints found!\n")
    return(data.frame())
  }
  
  # Prepare results dataframe
  results <- data.frame()
  
  # Process each patient - FIXED APPROACH
  for (i in 1:nrow(valid_patients)) {
    patient <- valid_patients$No_Patient[i]
    group <- valid_patients$Group[i]
    
    # Get all samples for this patient
    patient_indices <- which(meta_df$No_Patient == patient)
    
    if (length(patient_indices) != 2) {
      cat("  ✗ Patient", patient, "doesn't have exactly 2 samples\n")
      next
    }
    
    # Get sample names
    sample_names <- rownames(meta_df)[patient_indices]
    
    # Get timepoints for these samples
    timepoints <- meta_df$Timepoint[patient_indices]
    
    # Ensure we have one -1 and one -2
    if (!all(c("-1", "-2") %in% timepoints)) {
      cat("  ✗ Patient", patient, "doesn't have both timepoints\n")
      next
    }
    
    # Identify which sample is which timepoint
    sample_t1 <- sample_names[which(timepoints == "-1")]
    sample_t2 <- sample_names[which(timepoints == "-2")]
    
    if (length(sample_t1) != 1 || length(sample_t2) != 1) {
      cat("  ✗ Patient", patient, "timepoint identification failed\n")
      next
    }
    
    tryCatch({
      # Extract OTU data for these two samples directly from phyloseq
      otu_data <- as.data.frame(otu_table(ps_obj))
      
      # Get abundance vectors
      otu_t1 <- as.numeric(otu_data[, sample_t1])
      otu_t2 <- as.numeric(otu_data[, sample_t2])
      
      # Remove any NA values (replace with 0)
      otu_t1[is.na(otu_t1)] <- 0
      otu_t2[is.na(otu_t2)] <- 0
      
      # Calculate Bray-Curtis
      bc_distance <- calculate_bray_curtis_manual(otu_t1, otu_t2)
      
      if (!is.na(bc_distance)) {
        results <- rbind(results, data.frame(
          PatientID = patient,
          Group = group,
          BrayCurtis_Distance = bc_distance,
          stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        ))
        cat("  ✓ Patient", patient, "distance:", round(bc_distance, 4), "\n")
      } else {
        cat("  ✗ Patient", patient, "resulted in NA distance\n")
      }
    }, error = function(e) {
      cat("  ✗ Error for patient", patient, ":", e$message, "\n")
    })
  }
  
  cat("\nSuccessfully calculated distances for", nrow(results), "patients\n")
  return(results)
}

# Alternative: Even simpler version using matrix operations
calculate_within_sample_bray_curtis_simple <- function(ps_obj) {
  cat("=== Calculating Within-Sample Bray-Curtis Distances (Simple) ===\n")
  
  # Get OTU table as matrix
  otu_mat <- as.matrix(otu_table(ps_obj))
  
  # Get metadata
  meta_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj))
  
  # Create patient-timepoint mapping
  patient_map <- data.frame(
    SampleID = rownames(meta_df),
    PatientID = meta_df$No_Patient,
    Timepoint = meta_df$Timepoint,
    Group = meta_df$Group,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # Find patients with both timepoints
  patients_both <- patient_map %>%
    group_by(PatientID) %>%
    summarise(
      has_both = all(c("-1", "-2") %in% Timepoint),
      Group = first(Group)
    ) %>%
    filter(has_both)
  
  cat("Found", nrow(patients_both), "patients with both timepoints\n")
  
  if (nrow(patients_both) == 0) {
    return(data.frame())
  }
  
  # Prepare results
  results <- data.frame()
  
  for (i in 1:nrow(patients_both)) {
    patient <- patients_both$PatientID[i]
    group <- patients_both$Group[i]
    
    # Get samples for this patient
    patient_samples <- subset(patient_map, PatientID == patient)
    
    # Get sample IDs for each timepoint
    sample_t1 <- patient_samples$SampleID[patient_samples$Timepoint == "-1"]
    sample_t2 <- patient_samples$SampleID[patient_samples$Timepoint == "-2"]
    
    if (length(sample_t1) == 1 && length(sample_t2) == 1) {
      # Get abundance vectors
      vec1 <- otu_mat[, sample_t1]
      vec2 <- otu_mat[, sample_t2]
      
      # Convert to numeric and handle NAs
      vec1 <- as.numeric(vec1)
      vec2 <- as.numeric(vec2)
      vec1[is.na(vec1)] <- 0
      vec2[is.na(vec2)] <- 0
      
      # Calculate distance
      bc_dist <- calculate_bray_curtis_manual(vec1, vec2)
      
      if (!is.na(bc_dist)) {
        results <- rbind(results, data.frame(
          PatientID = patient,
          Group = group,
          BrayCurtis_Distance = bc_dist,
          stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        ))
        cat("  ✓ Patient", patient, "distance:", round(bc_dist, 4), "\n")
      }
    }
  }
  
  return(results)
}

# Manual Bray-Curtis calculation (more reliable)
calculate_bray_curtis_manual <- function(vec1, vec2) {
  # Bray-Curtis formula: sum(|x_i - y_i|) / sum(x_i + y_i)
  numerator <- sum(abs(vec1 - vec2), na.rm = TRUE)
  denominator <- sum(vec1 + vec2, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  if (denominator == 0) {
    return(NA)  # Avoid division by zero
  }
  
  return(numerator / denominator)
}

# Plot the results
plot_bray_curtis_by_group <- function(distance_df, output_dir) {
  if (nrow(distance_df) == 0) {
    cat("No data to plot!\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Ensure Group is factor
  distance_df$Group <- factor(distance_df$Group)
  
  # Define colors
  group_colors <- c("A" = "#FF3333", "B" = "#3366FF", "C" = "#33CC33")
  
  # Create plot
  p <- ggplot(distance_df, aes(x = Group, y = BrayCurtis_Distance, fill = Group)) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, size = 2, alpha = 0.6, color = "black") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = group_colors) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
    labs(
      title = "Within-Sample Bray-Curtis Distances Between Timepoints",
      subtitle = "How similar are samples to themselves at different timepoints?",
      x = "Group",
      y = "Bray-Curtis Distance (0 = identical, 1 = completely different)",
      caption = paste("N =", nrow(distance_df), "patients with both timepoints")
    ) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
      legend.position = "none"
    ) +
    ylim(0, 1)
  
  # Add statistical comparisons if possible
  groups <- unique(distance_df$Group)
  if (length(groups) >= 2) {
    comparisons <- combn(groups, 2, simplify = FALSE)
    
    # Filter comparisons where both groups have at least 2 observations
    valid_comparisons <- list()
    for (comp in comparisons) {
      group1_count <- sum(distance_df$Group == comp[1])
      group2_count <- sum(distance_df$Group == comp[2])
      if (group1_count >= 2 && group2_count >= 2) {
        valid_comparisons <- c(valid_comparisons, list(comp))
      }
    }
    
    if (length(valid_comparisons) > 0) {
      p <- p + stat_compare_means(
        comparisons = valid_comparisons,
        method = "wilcox.test",
        label = "p.signif",
        size = 5,
        tip.length = 0.02
      )
    }
  }
  
  # Save plot
  dir.create(output_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
  plot_file <- file.path(output_dir, "within_sample_bray_curtis.png")
  ggsave(plot_file, p, width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 300)
  cat("Saved plot to:", plot_file, "\n")
  
  return(p)
}

# Save results
save_bray_curtis_results <- function(distance_df, output_dir) {
  dir.create(output_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
  
  # Save raw distances
  distances_file <- file.path(output_dir, "bray_curtis_distances.csv")
  write.csv(distance_df, distances_file, row.names = FALSE)
  cat("Saved distances to:", distances_file, "\n")
  
  # Save summary statistics
  summary_stats <- distance_df %>%
    group_by(Group) %>%
    summarise(
      N = n(),
      Mean = mean(BrayCurtis_Distance),
      SD = sd(BrayCurtis_Distance),
      Median = median(BrayCurtis_Distance),
      Min = min(BrayCurtis_Distance),
      Max = max(BrayCurtis_Distance)
    )
  
  stats_file <- file.path(output_dir, "bray_curtis_summary.csv")
  write.csv(summary_stats, stats_file, row.names = FALSE)
  cat("Saved summary statistics to:", stats_file, "\n")
  
  # Perform statistical tests
  if (length(unique(distance_df$Group)) >= 2) {
    test_results <- data.frame()
    groups <- unique(distance_df$Group)
    comparisons <- combn(groups, 2, simplify = FALSE)
    
    for (comp in comparisons) {
      group1 <- comp[1]
      group2 <- comp[2]
      
      data1 <- distance_df$BrayCurtis_Distance[distance_df$Group == group1]
      data2 <- distance_df$BrayCurtis_Distance[distance_df$Group == group2]
      
      if (length(data1) >= 2 && length(data2) >= 2) {
        test <- wilcox.test(data1, data2, exact = FALSE)
        
        test_results <- rbind(test_results, data.frame(
          Comparison = paste(group1, "vs", group2),
          N_Group1 = length(data1),
          N_Group2 = length(data2),
          Mean_Group1 = mean(data1),
          Mean_Group2 = mean(data2),
          P_value = test$p.value,
          P_value_rounded = round(test$p.value, 4),
          Significance = ifelse(test$p.value < 0.001, "***",
                               ifelse(test$p.value < 0.01, "**",
                                      ifelse(test$p.value < 0.05, "*", "ns")))
        ))
      }
    }
    
    if (nrow(test_results) > 0) {
      tests_file <- file.path(output_dir, "bray_curtis_statistics.csv")
      write.csv(test_results, tests_file, row.names = FALSE)
      cat("Saved statistical tests to:", tests_file, "\n")
    }
  }
}

# MAIN FUNCTION - UPDATED
analyze_within_sample_similarity <- function(ps_obj, output_dir = "within_sample_analysis") {
  cat("=== WITHIN-SAMPLE SIMILARITY ANALYSIS ===\n")
  
  # Optional: Convert to relative abundance first
  cat("\nConverting to relative abundance...\n")
  ps_rel <- transform_sample_counts(ps_obj, function(x) x / sum(x))
  
  # Calculate distances using simple method
  cat("\nCalculating Bray-Curtis distances...\n")
  distances <- calculate_within_sample_bray_curtis_simple(ps_rel)
  
  if (nrow(distances) == 0) {
    cat("\nNo valid distances calculated. Analysis stopped.\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Create plot
  cat("\nCreating visualization...\n")
  plot <- plot_bray_curtis_by_group(distances, output_dir)
  
  # Save results
  cat("\nSaving results...\n")
  save_bray_curtis_results(distances, output_dir)
  
  # Print summary
  cat("\n=== ANALYSIS SUMMARY ===\n")
  cat("Total patients analyzed:", nrow(distances), "\n")
  cat("\nSummary by group:\n")
  print(distances %>%
    group_by(Group) %>%
    summarise(
      N = n(),
      Mean_Distance = round(mean(BrayCurtis_Distance), 4),
      SD = round(sd(BrayCurtis_Distance), 4),
      Median = round(median(BrayCurtis_Distance), 4)
    ))
  
  cat("\n=== COMPLETE ===\n")
  cat("Results saved in:", output_dir, "\n")
  
  return(list(
    distances = distances,
    plot = plot
  ))
}

# RUN ANALYSIS
# Use the simple version
results <- analyze_within_sample_similarity(
  ps_obj = ps,
  output_dir = "/home/sonec_iv/within_sample_analysis"
)
```

# upd
```{r}
# Enhanced plotting function with improved significance display
#ps <- readRDS('/home/sonec_iv/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_all_physeq_wgs_COUNTS_v2.rds')
plot_bray_curtis_by_group <- function(distance_df, output_dir, 
                                       test_method = "wilcox.test",
                                       show_p_values = FALSE,
                                       hide_ns = FALSE,
                                       step_increase = 0.08,
                                       y_max = 1.2) {
  if (nrow(distance_df) == 0) {
    cat("No data to plot!\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Ensure Group is factor
  distance_df$Group <- factor(distance_df$Group)
  
  # Define colors
  group_colors <- c("A" = "#FF3333", "B" = "#3366FF", "C" = "#33CC33")
  
  # Create plot
  p <- ggplot(distance_df, aes(x = Group, y = BrayCurtis_Distance, fill = Group)) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.6) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, size = 2, alpha = 0.6, color = "black", height = 0) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = group_colors) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
    labs(
      title = "Within-Sample Bray-Curtis Distances Between Timepoints",
      subtitle = "How similar are samples to themselves at different timepoints?",
      x = "Group",
      y = "Bray-Curtis Distance (0 = identical, 1 = completely different)",
      caption = paste("N =", nrow(distance_df), "patients with both timepoints")
    ) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
      legend.position = "none",
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank()
    ) +
    ylim(0, y_max)
  
  # Prepare comparisons
  groups <- unique(distance_df$Group)
  
  if (length(groups) >= 2) {
    # Generate all possible pairwise comparisons
    comparisons <- combn(groups, 2, simplify = FALSE)
    
    # Filter comparisons where both groups have at least 2 observations
    valid_comparisons <- list()
    for (comp in comparisons) {
      group1_count <- sum(distance_df$Group == comp[1])
      group2_count <- sum(distance_df$Group == comp[2])
      if (group1_count >= 2 && group2_count >= 2) {
        valid_comparisons <- c(valid_comparisons, list(comp))
      }
    }
    
    if (length(valid_comparisons) > 0) {
      # OPTION 1: Show significance stars (default)
      if (!show_p_values) {
        p <- p + stat_compare_means(
          comparisons = valid_comparisons,
          method = test_method,
          label = "p.signif",
          size = 6,
          tip.length = 0.02,
          step.increase = step_increase,
          hide.ns = hide_ns,
          vjust = 0.5,
          bracket.size = 0.8
        )
      } 
      # OPTION 2: Show actual p-values
      else {
        p <- p + stat_compare_means(
          comparisons = valid_comparisons,
          method = test_method,
          label = "p.format",  # Shows formatted p-value
          size = 4.5,
          tip.length = 0.02,
          step.increase = step_increase,
          bracket.size = 0.8
        )
      }
      
      # Add annotation for test method used
      p <- p + labs(caption = paste(
        p$labels$caption,
        paste0("\nTest: ", test_method, ifelse(hide_ns, " (ns hidden)", ""))
      ))
    }
  }
  
  # Save plot
  dir.create(output_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
  plot_file <- file.path(output_dir, "within_sample_bray_curtis.png")
  ggsave(plot_file, p, width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 300)
  cat("Saved plot to:", plot_file, "\n")
  
  return(p)
}

# Alternative: Custom significance annotations with more control
plot_bray_curtis_custom <- function(distance_df, output_dir) {
  if (nrow(distance_df) == 0) {
    cat("No data to plot!\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Ensure Group is factor
  distance_df$Group <- factor(distance_df$Group)
  
  # Define colors
  group_colors <- c("A" = "#FF3333", "B" = "#3366FF", "C" = "#33CC33")
  
  # Perform statistical tests first
  test_results <- perform_pairwise_tests(distance_df)
  
  # Create base plot
  p <- ggplot(distance_df, aes(x = Group, y = BrayCurtis_Distance, fill = Group)) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.6) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, size = 2, alpha = 0.6, color = "black", height = 0) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = group_colors) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
    labs(
      title = "Within-Sample Bray-Curtis Distances Between Timepoints",
      subtitle = "How similar are samples to themselves at different timepoints?",
      x = "Group",
      y = "Bray-Curtis Distance (0 = identical, 1 = completely different)",
      caption = paste("N =", nrow(distance_df), "patients with both timepoints")
    ) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
      legend.position = "none",
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
    ) +
    ylim(0, 1.1)
  
  # Add custom significance annotations if tests were performed
  if (nrow(test_results) > 0) {
    # Calculate y positions for brackets
    max_val <- max(distance_df$BrayCurtis_Distance, na.rm = TRUE)
    y_step <- max_val * 0.05
    y_positions <- seq(max_val + y_step, 
                       max_val + (nrow(test_results) * y_step), 
                       by = y_step)
    
    # Add significance annotations
    for (i in 1:nrow(test_results)) {
      comp <- test_results[i, ]
      
      # Add significance stars
      p <- p + annotate("text",
        x = mean(c(which(levels(distance_df$Group) == comp$Group1),
                   which(levels(distance_df$Group) == comp$Group2))),
        y = y_positions[i],
        label = comp$Significance,
        size = 8,
        fontface = "bold"
      )
      
      # Add bracket line
      p <- p + annotate("segment",
        x = which(levels(distance_df$Group) == comp$Group1),
        xend = which(levels(distance_df$Group) == comp$Group2),
        y = y_positions[i] - (y_step * 0.2),
        yend = y_positions[i] - (y_step * 0.2),
        size = 0.8
      )
      
      # Add vertical lines at ends
      p <- p + annotate("segment",
        x = which(levels(distance_df$Group) == comp$Group1),
        xend = which(levels(distance_df$Group) == comp$Group1),
        y = y_positions[i] - (y_step * 0.2),
        yend = y_positions[i] - (y_step * 0.3),
        size = 0.8
      )
      
      p <- p + annotate("segment",
        x = which(levels(distance_df$Group) == comp$Group2),
        xend = which(levels(distance_df$Group) == comp$Group2),
        y = y_positions[i] - (y_step * 0.2),
        yend = y_positions[i] - (y_step * 0.3),
        size = 0.8
      )
    }
    
    # Adjust y limit to accommodate significance annotations
    p <- p + ylim(0, max(y_positions) + y_step)
  }
  
  # Save plot
  dir.create(output_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
  plot_file <- file.path(output_dir, "within_sample_bray_curtis_custom.png")
  ggsave(plot_file, p, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)
  cat("Saved custom plot to:", plot_file, "\n")
  
  return(p)
}

# Helper function for pairwise tests
perform_pairwise_tests <- function(distance_df) {
  results <- data.frame()
  groups <- unique(distance_df$Group)
  comparisons <- combn(groups, 2, simplify = FALSE)
  
  for (comp in comparisons) {
    group1 <- comp[1]
    group2 <- comp[2]
    
    data1 <- distance_df$BrayCurtis_Distance[distance_df$Group == group1]
    data2 <- distance_df$BrayCurtis_Distance[distance_df$Group == group2]
    
    if (length(data1) >= 2 && length(data2) >= 2) {
      test <- wilcox.test(data1, data2, exact = FALSE)
      
      results <- rbind(results, data.frame(
        Group1 = group1,
        Group2 = group2,
        P_value = test$p.value,
        Significance = ifelse(test$p.value < 0.001, "***",
                             ifelse(test$p.value < 0.01, "**",
                                    ifelse(test$p.value < 0.05, "*", "ns")))
      ))
    }
  }
  
  return(results)
}

# Updated main function with options
analyze_within_sample_similarity <- function(ps_obj, output_dir = "within_sample_analysis",
                                             show_p_values = FALSE,
                                             hide_ns = TRUE,
                                             custom_plot = FALSE) {
  cat("=== WITHIN-SAMPLE SIMILARITY ANALYSIS ===\n")
  
  # Optional: Convert to relative abundance first
  cat("\nConverting to relative abundance...\n")
  ps_rel <- transform_sample_counts(ps_obj, function(x) x / sum(x))
  
  # Calculate distances using simple method
  cat("\nCalculating Bray-Curtis distances...\n")
  distances <- calculate_within_sample_bray_curtis_simple(ps_rel)
  
  if (nrow(distances) == 0) {
    cat("\nNo valid distances calculated. Analysis stopped.\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Create plot
  cat("\nCreating visualization...\n")
  if (custom_plot) {
    plot <- plot_bray_curtis_custom(distances, output_dir)
  } else {
    plot <- plot_bray_curtis_by_group(distances, output_dir, 
                                      show_p_values = show_p_values,
                                      hide_ns = hide_ns)
  }
  
  # Save results
  cat("\nSaving results...\n")
  save_bray_curtis_results(distances, output_dir)
  
  # Print summary
  cat("\n=== ANALYSIS SUMMARY ===\n")
  cat("Total patients analyzed:", nrow(distances), "\n")
  cat("\nSummary by group:\n")
  print(distances %>%
    group_by(Group) %>%
    summarise(
      N = n(),
      Mean_Distance = round(mean(BrayCurtis_Distance), 4),
      SD = round(sd(BrayCurtis_Distance), 4),
      Median = round(median(BrayCurtis_Distance), 4)
    ))
  
  cat("\n=== COMPLETE ===\n")
  cat("Results saved in:", output_dir, "\n")
  
  return(list(
    distances = distances,
    plot = plot
  ))
}

# RUN ANALYSIS WITH DIFFERENT OPTIONS
# Option 1: Default (significance stars)
results1 <- analyze_within_sample_similarity(
  ps_obj = ps,
  output_dir = "/home/sonec_iv/within_sample_analysis"
)

# Option 2: Show actual p-values
results2 <- analyze_within_sample_similarity(
  ps_obj = ps,
  output_dir = "/home/sonec_iv/within_sample_analysis_pvalues",
  show_p_values = TRUE
)

# Option 3: Custom plot
results3 <- analyze_within_sample_similarity(
  ps_obj = ps,
  output_dir = "/home/sonec_iv/within_sample_analysis_custom",
  custom_plot = TRUE
)

# Option 4: Hide non-significant results
results4 <- analyze_within_sample_similarity(
  ps_obj = ps,
  output_dir = "/home/sonec_iv/within_sample_analysis_ns_hidden",
  hide_ns = TRUE
)
```
#final?
```{r}
# ====================================================
# WITHIN-SAMPLE BRAY-CURTIS ANALYSIS WITH SIGNIFICANCE STARS
# ====================================================

# Load required libraries
library(phyloseq)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(ggpubr)  # for stat_compare_means

# ----------------------------------------------------
# 1. Load the phyloseq object
# ----------------------------------------------------
ps <- readRDS('/home/sonec_iv/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_all_physeq_wgs_COUNTS_v2.rds')
#ps <- readRDS('/home/sonec_iv/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_RESEQUENCING_wgs.rds')

bad_batch_samples <- c("AO-203-2", "AO-207-1", "B-022-2", "MB124-2")

if (length(bad_batch_samples) > 0) {
  ps <- prune_samples(!(sample_names(ps) %in% bad_batch_samples), ps)
  cat("Removed", length(bad_batch_samples), "samples\n")
}


# ----------------------------------------------------
# 2. Helper function: manual Bray-Curtis calculation
# ----------------------------------------------------
calculate_bray_curtis_manual <- function(vec1, vec2) {
  numerator <- sum(abs(vec1 - vec2), na.rm = TRUE)
  denominator <- sum(vec1 + vec2, na.rm = TRUE)
  if (denominator == 0) return(NA)
  return(numerator / denominator)
}

# ----------------------------------------------------
# 3. Calculate within-sample Bray-Curtis distances (simple version)
# ----------------------------------------------------
calculate_within_sample_bray_curtis_simple <- function(ps_obj) {
  cat("=== Calculating Within-Sample Bray-Curtis Distances ===\n")
  
  otu_mat <- as.matrix(otu_table(ps_obj))
  meta_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj))
  
  patient_map <- data.frame(
    SampleID = rownames(meta_df),
    PatientID = meta_df$No_Patient,
    Timepoint = meta_df$Timepoint,
    Group = meta_df$Group,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  patients_both <- patient_map %>%
    group_by(PatientID) %>%
    summarise(
      has_both = all(c("-1", "-2") %in% Timepoint),
      Group = first(Group)
    ) %>%
    filter(has_both)
  
  cat("Found", nrow(patients_both), "patients with both timepoints\n")
  
  if (nrow(patients_both) == 0) return(data.frame())
  
  results <- data.frame()
  
  for (i in 1:nrow(patients_both)) {
    patient <- patients_both$PatientID[i]
    group   <- patients_both$Group[i]
    
    patient_samples <- subset(patient_map, PatientID == patient)
    sample_t1 <- patient_samples$SampleID[patient_samples$Timepoint == "-1"]
    sample_t2 <- patient_samples$SampleID[patient_samples$Timepoint == "-2"]
    
    if (length(sample_t1) == 1 && length(sample_t2) == 1) {
      vec1 <- as.numeric(otu_mat[, sample_t1])
      vec2 <- as.numeric(otu_mat[, sample_t2])
      vec1[is.na(vec1)] <- 0
      vec2[is.na(vec2)] <- 0
      
      bc_dist <- calculate_bray_curtis_manual(vec1, vec2)
      
      if (!is.na(bc_dist)) {
        results <- rbind(results, data.frame(
          PatientID = patient,
          Group = group,
          BrayCurtis_Distance = bc_dist,
          stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        ))
        cat("  ✓ Patient", patient, "distance:", round(bc_dist, 4), "\n")
      }
    }
  }
  
  cat("\nSuccessfully calculated distances for", nrow(results), "patients\n")
  return(results)
}

# ----------------------------------------------------
# 4. Enhanced plotting function with significance stars
# ----------------------------------------------------
plot_bray_curtis_by_group <- function(distance_df, output_dir, 
                                       test_method = "wilcox.test",
                                       show_p_values = FALSE,
                                       step_increase = 0.08,
                                       y_max = 1.1) {
  if (nrow(distance_df) == 0) {
    cat("No data to plot!\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  distance_df$Group <- factor(distance_df$Group)
  
  # Define colors (adjust if you have more/different groups)
  group_colors <- c("A" = "#FF3333", "B" = "#3366FF", "C" = "#33CC33")
  
  p <- ggplot(distance_df, aes(x = Group, y = BrayCurtis_Distance, fill = Group)) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.6) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, size = 2, alpha = 0.6, color = "black", height = 0) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = group_colors) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
    labs(
      title = "Within-Sample Bray-Curtis Distances Between Timepoints",
      #subtitle = "How similar are samples to themselves at different timepoints?",
      x = "Group",
      y = "Bray-Curtis Distance",
      #caption = paste("N =", nrow(distance_df), "patients with both timepoints")
    ) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
      legend.position = "none",
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank()
    ) +
    ylim(0, y_max)
  
  # Prepare pairwise comparisons
  groups <- unique(distance_df$Group)
  
  if (length(groups) >= 2) {
    comparisons <- combn(groups, 2, simplify = FALSE)
    
    # Keep only comparisons where both groups have at least 2 observations
    valid_comparisons <- list()
    for (comp in comparisons) {
      group1_count <- sum(distance_df$Group == comp[1])
      group2_count <- sum(distance_df$Group == comp[2])
      if (group1_count >= 2 && group2_count >= 2) {
        valid_comparisons <- c(valid_comparisons, list(comp))
      }
    }
    
    if (length(valid_comparisons) > 0) {
      if (!show_p_values) {
        # Show significance stars (default)
        p <- p + stat_compare_means(
          comparisons = valid_comparisons,
          method = test_method,
          label = "p.signif",
          size = 6,
          tip.length = 0.02,
          step.increase = step_increase,
          vjust = 0.5,
          bracket.size = 0.8
        )
      } else {
        # Show actual p-values
        p <- p + stat_compare_means(
          comparisons = valid_comparisons,
          method = test_method,
          label = "p.format",
          size = 4.5,
          tip.length = 0.02,
          step.increase = step_increase,
          bracket.size = 0.8
        )
      }
      
      # Add test method to caption
      p <- p + labs(caption = paste(p$labels$caption, paste0("\nTest: ", test_method)))
    }
  }
  
  # Save plot
  dir.create(output_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
  plot_file <- file.path(output_dir, "within_sample_bray_curtis.png")
  ggsave(plot_file, p, width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 300)
  cat("Saved plot to:", plot_file, "\n")
  
  return(p)
}

# ----------------------------------------------------
# 5. Save results (distances, summary, statistics)
# ----------------------------------------------------
save_bray_curtis_results <- function(distance_df, output_dir) {
  dir.create(output_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
  
  # Raw distances
  distances_file <- file.path(output_dir, "bray_curtis_distances.csv")
  write.csv(distance_df, distances_file, row.names = FALSE)
  cat("Saved distances to:", distances_file, "\n")
  
  # Summary statistics per group
  summary_stats <- distance_df %>%
    group_by(Group) %>%
    summarise(
      N = n(),
      Mean = mean(BrayCurtis_Distance),
      SD = sd(BrayCurtis_Distance),
      Median = median(BrayCurtis_Distance),
      Min = min(BrayCurtis_Distance),
      Max = max(BrayCurtis_Distance)
    )
  stats_file <- file.path(output_dir, "bray_curtis_summary.csv")
  write.csv(summary_stats, stats_file, row.names = FALSE)
  cat("Saved summary statistics to:", stats_file, "\n")
  
  # Pairwise statistical tests
  if (length(unique(distance_df$Group)) >= 2) {
    test_results <- data.frame()
    groups <- unique(distance_df$Group)
    comparisons <- combn(groups, 2, simplify = FALSE)
    
    for (comp in comparisons) {
      group1 <- comp[1]
      group2 <- comp[2]
      
      data1 <- distance_df$BrayCurtis_Distance[distance_df$Group == group1]
      data2 <- distance_df$BrayCurtis_Distance[distance_df$Group == group2]
      
      if (length(data1) >= 2 && length(data2) >= 2) {
        test <- wilcox.test(data1, data2, exact = FALSE)
        
        test_results <- rbind(test_results, data.frame(
          Comparison = paste(group1, "vs", group2),
          N_Group1 = length(data1),
          N_Group2 = length(data2),
          Mean_Group1 = mean(data1),
          Mean_Group2 = mean(data2),
          P_value = test$p.value,
          P_value_rounded = round(test$p.value, 4),
          Significance = ifelse(test$p.value < 0.001, "***",
                               ifelse(test$p.value < 0.01, "**",
                                      ifelse(test$p.value < 0.05, "*", "ns")))
        ))
      }
    }
    
    if (nrow(test_results) > 0) {
      tests_file <- file.path(output_dir, "bray_curtis_statistics.csv")
      write.csv(test_results, tests_file, row.names = FALSE)
      cat("Saved statistical tests to:", tests_file, "\n")
    }
  }
}

# ----------------------------------------------------
# 6. Main analysis wrapper
# ----------------------------------------------------
analyze_within_sample_similarity <- function(ps_obj, output_dir = "within_sample_analysis",
                                             show_p_values = FALSE) {
  cat("=== WITHIN-SAMPLE SIMILARITY ANALYSIS ===\n")
  
  # Convert to relative abundance (optional but recommended)
  cat("\nConverting to relative abundance...\n")
  ps_rel <- transform_sample_counts(ps_obj, function(x) x / sum(x))
  
  # Calculate distances
  cat("\nCalculating Bray-Curtis distances...\n")
  distances <- calculate_within_sample_bray_curtis_simple(ps_rel)
  
  if (nrow(distances) == 0) {
    cat("\nNo valid distances calculated. Analysis stopped.\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Create plot
  cat("\nCreating visualization...\n")
  plot <- plot_bray_curtis_by_group(distances, output_dir, 
                                    show_p_values = show_p_values)
  
  # Save results
  cat("\nSaving results...\n")
  save_bray_curtis_results(distances, output_dir)
  
  # Print summary to console
  cat("\n=== ANALYSIS SUMMARY ===\n")
  cat("Total patients analyzed:", nrow(distances), "\n")
  cat("\nSummary by group:\n")
  print(distances %>%
          group_by(Group) %>%
          summarise(
            N = n(),
            Mean_Distance = round(mean(BrayCurtis_Distance), 4),
            SD = round(sd(BrayCurtis_Distance), 4),
            Median = round(median(BrayCurtis_Distance), 4)
          ))
  
  cat("\n=== COMPLETE ===\n")
  cat("Results saved in:", output_dir, "\n")
  
  return(list(distances = distances, plot = plot))
}

# ----------------------------------------------------
# 7. RUN THE ANALYSIS
# ----------------------------------------------------
# Use default: significance stars on plot
results <- analyze_within_sample_similarity(
  ps_obj = ps,
  output_dir = "/home/sonec_iv/RESEQ_within_sample_analysis"
)

# If you prefer to see actual p-values instead of stars, run:
# results <- analyze_within_sample_similarity(
#   ps_obj = ps,
#   output_dir = "/home/sonec_iv/within_sample_analysis_pvalues",
#   show_p_values = TRUE
# )
```

# add grouping
```{r}
# ====================================================
# WITHIN-SAMPLE BRAY-CURTIS ANALYSIS WITH SIGNIFICANCE STARS
# AND STRATIFICATION BY ADDITIONAL CATEGORICAL VARIABLES
# ====================================================


# ----------------------------------------------------
# 1. Load the phyloseq object
# ----------------------------------------------------
ps <- readRDS('/home/sonec_iv/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_all_physeq_wgs_COUNTS_v2.rds')
#ps <- readRDS('/home/sonec_iv/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_ps_mags.rds')
ps@sam_data$Batch <- as.factor(ps@sam_data$Batch)

bad_batch_samples <- c("AO-203-2", "AO-207-1", "B-022-2", "MB124-2")

if (length(bad_batch_samples) > 0) {
  ps <- prune_samples(!(sample_names(ps) %in% bad_batch_samples), ps)
  cat("Removed", length(bad_batch_samples), "samples\n")
}

# ----------------------------------------------------
# 2. Helper function: manual Bray-Curtis calculation
# ----------------------------------------------------
calculate_bray_curtis_manual <- function(vec1, vec2) {
  numerator <- sum(abs(vec1 - vec2), na.rm = TRUE)
  denominator <- sum(vec1 + vec2, na.rm = TRUE)
  if (denominator == 0) return(NA)
  return(numerator / denominator)
}

# ----------------------------------------------------
# 3. Calculate within-sample Bray-Curtis distances (simple version)
# ----------------------------------------------------
calculate_within_sample_bray_curtis_simple <- function(ps_obj) {
  cat("=== Calculating Within-Sample Bray-Curtis Distances ===\n")
  
  otu_mat <- as.matrix(otu_table(ps_obj))
  meta_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj))
  
  patient_map <- data.frame(
    SampleID = rownames(meta_df),
    PatientID = meta_df$No_Patient,
    Timepoint = meta_df$Timepoint,
    Group = meta_df$Group,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  patients_both <- patient_map %>%
    group_by(PatientID) %>%
    summarise(
      has_both = all(c("-1", "-2") %in% Timepoint),
      Group = first(Group)
    ) %>%
    filter(has_both)
  
  cat("Found", nrow(patients_both), "patients with both timepoints\n")
  
  if (nrow(patients_both) == 0) return(data.frame())
  
  results <- data.frame()
  
  for (i in 1:nrow(patients_both)) {
    patient <- patients_both$PatientID[i]
    group   <- patients_both$Group[i]
    
    patient_samples <- subset(patient_map, PatientID == patient)
    sample_t1 <- patient_samples$SampleID[patient_samples$Timepoint == "-1"]
    sample_t2 <- patient_samples$SampleID[patient_samples$Timepoint == "-2"]
    
    if (length(sample_t1) == 1 && length(sample_t2) == 1) {
      vec1 <- as.numeric(otu_mat[, sample_t1])
      vec2 <- as.numeric(otu_mat[, sample_t2])
      vec1[is.na(vec1)] <- 0
      vec2[is.na(vec2)] <- 0
      
      bc_dist <- calculate_bray_curtis_manual(vec1, vec2)
      
      if (!is.na(bc_dist)) {
        results <- rbind(results, data.frame(
          PatientID = patient,
          Group = group,
          BrayCurtis_Distance = bc_dist,
          stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        ))
        cat("  ✓ Patient", patient, "distance:", round(bc_dist, 4), "\n")
      }
    }
  }
  
  cat("\nSuccessfully calculated distances for", nrow(results), "patients\n")
  return(results)
}

# ----------------------------------------------------
# 4. Get patient-level metadata (one row per patient)
# ----------------------------------------------------
get_patient_metadata <- function(ps_obj) {
  meta <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj))
  # Take first occurrence for each patient (assuming patient-level variables are constant)
  patient_meta <- meta %>%
    group_by(No_Patient) %>%
    slice(1) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    select(-SampleID, -Timepoint)  # remove sample-specific columns if present
  return(patient_meta)
}

# ----------------------------------------------------
# 5. Enhanced plotting function with significance stars (for a single subset)
# ----------------------------------------------------
plot_bray_curtis_by_group <- function(distance_df, output_dir, 
                                       test_method = "wilcox.test",
                                       show_p_values = FALSE,
                                       step_increase = 0.08,
                                       y_max = 1.1,
                                       plot_title = NULL,
                                       filename_suffix = "") {
  if (nrow(distance_df) == 0) {
    cat("No data to plot!\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  distance_df$Group <- factor(distance_df$Group)
  
  # Define colors (adjust if you have more/different groups)
  group_colors <- c("A" = "#FF3333", "B" = "#3366FF", "C" = "#33CC33")
  
  if (is.null(plot_title)) {
    plot_title <- "Within-Sample Bray-Curtis Distances Between Timepoints"
  }
  
  p <- ggplot(distance_df, aes(x = Group, y = BrayCurtis_Distance, fill = Group)) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.6) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, size = 2, alpha = 0.6, color = "black", height = 0) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = group_colors) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
    labs(
      title = plot_title,
      subtitle = "How similar are samples to themselves at different timepoints?",
      x = "Group",
      y = "Bray-Curtis Distance (0 = identical, 1 = completely different)",
      #caption = paste("N =", nrow(distance_df), "patients")
    ) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
      legend.position = "none",
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank()
    ) +
    ylim(0, y_max)
  
  # Prepare pairwise comparisons
  groups <- unique(distance_df$Group)
  
  if (length(groups) >= 2) {
    comparisons <- combn(groups, 2, simplify = FALSE)
    
    # Keep only comparisons where both groups have at least 2 observations
    valid_comparisons <- list()
    for (comp in comparisons) {
      group1_count <- sum(distance_df$Group == comp[1])
      group2_count <- sum(distance_df$Group == comp[2])
      if (group1_count >= 2 && group2_count >= 2) {
        valid_comparisons <- c(valid_comparisons, list(comp))
      }
    }
    
    if (length(valid_comparisons) > 0) {
      if (!show_p_values) {
        # Show significance stars (default)
        p <- p + stat_compare_means(
          comparisons = valid_comparisons,
          method = test_method,
          label = "p.signif",
          size = 6,
          tip.length = 0.02,
          step.increase = step_increase,
          vjust = 0.5,
          bracket.size = 0.8
        )
      } else {
        # Show actual p-values
        p <- p + stat_compare_means(
          comparisons = valid_comparisons,
          method = test_method,
          label = "p.format",
          size = 4.5,
          tip.length = 0.02,
          step.increase = step_increase,
          bracket.size = 0.8
        )
      }
      
      # Add test method to caption
      p <- p + labs(caption = paste(p$labels$caption, paste0("\nTest: ", test_method)))
    }
  }
  
  # Save plot
  dir.create(output_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
  if (filename_suffix != "") {
    filename_suffix <- paste0("_", filename_suffix)
  }
  plot_file <- file.path(output_dir, paste0("within_sample_bray_curtis", filename_suffix, ".png"))
  ggsave(plot_file, p, width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 300)
  cat("Saved plot to:", plot_file, "\n")
  
  return(p)
}

# ----------------------------------------------------
# 6. Function to generate plots stratified by a categorical variable
# ----------------------------------------------------
plot_bray_curtis_by_categorical <- function(distance_df, patient_meta, 
                                            cat_var, output_dir,
                                            show_p_values = FALSE) {
  
  cat("\n--- Processing categorical variable:", cat_var, "---\n")
  
  # Select only the relevant categorical variable from patient_meta
  if (!"No_Patient" %in% colnames(patient_meta)) {
    cat("Error: No_Patient column not found in patient_meta\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  patient_subset <- patient_meta %>% 
    select(No_Patient, !!sym(cat_var)) %>%
    drop_na(!!sym(cat_var))  # remove patients with NA for this variable
  
  # Merge distances with patient subset
  df_merged <- distance_df %>%
    left_join(patient_subset, by = c("PatientID" = "No_Patient"))
  
  # Check if merge was successful
  if (!cat_var %in% colnames(df_merged)) {
    cat("Warning: Variable", cat_var, "not found after merge. Skipping.\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Remove rows with NA in this variable (again, just in case)
  df_merged <- df_merged %>% drop_na(!!sym(cat_var))
  
  if (nrow(df_merged) == 0) {
    cat("No data after removing NAs. Skipping.\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Get levels of the categorical variable
  levels_vec <- unique(df_merged[[cat_var]])
  levels_vec <- levels_vec[!is.na(levels_vec)]
  
  if (length(levels_vec) == 0) {
    cat("No levels found. Skipping.\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Create subdirectory for this variable
  var_dir <- file.path(output_dir, paste0("by_", cat_var))
  dir.create(var_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
  
  # For each level, generate a separate plot
  for (lev in levels_vec) {
    subset_df <- df_merged %>% filter(!!sym(cat_var) == lev)
    
    if (nrow(subset_df) < 3) {  # need at least some data to plot
      cat("  Skipping level", lev, "- only", nrow(subset_df), "patients\n")
      next
    }
    
    # Create a safe filename part
    safe_lev <- gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9]", "_", as.character(lev))
    filename_suffix <- paste0(cat_var, "_", safe_lev)
    
    plot_title <- paste0("Within-Sample Bray-Curtis Distances\n",
                         cat_var, " = ", lev)
    
    plot_bray_curtis_by_group(
      distance_df = subset_df,
      output_dir = var_dir,
      show_p_values = show_p_values,
      plot_title = plot_title,
      filename_suffix = filename_suffix
    )
  }
  
  # Optionally, create a faceted plot with all levels together
  if (length(levels_vec) > 1) {
    # Create a faceted plot
    df_merged$Group <- factor(df_merged$Group)
    df_merged[[cat_var]] <- factor(df_merged[[cat_var]])
    
    # Define colors
    group_colors <- c("A" = "#FF3333", "B" = "#3366FF", "C" = "#33CC33")
    
    p_facet <- ggplot(df_merged, aes(x = Group, y = BrayCurtis_Distance, fill = Group)) +
      geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.6) +
      geom_jitter(width = 0.2, size = 2, alpha = 0.6, color = "black", height = 0) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = group_colors) +
      facet_wrap(as.formula(paste("~", cat_var)), nrow = 1) +
      theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
      labs(
        title = paste("Within-Sample Bray-Curtis Distances by", cat_var),
        x = "Group",
        y = "Bray-Curtis Distance",
        #caption = paste("N =", nrow(df_merged), "patients")
      ) +
      theme(
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
        legend.position = "none",
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "lightgray"),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold")
      ) +
      ylim(0, 1.1)
    
    facet_file <- file.path(var_dir, paste0("facet_", cat_var, ".png"))
    ggsave(facet_file, p_facet, width = 5 * length(levels_vec), height = 7, dpi = 300, limitsize = FALSE)
    cat("Saved faceted plot to:", facet_file, "\n")
  }
}


# ----------------------------------------------------
# 7. Save results (distances, summary, statistics)
# ----------------------------------------------------
save_bray_curtis_results <- function(distance_df, output_dir) {
  dir.create(output_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
  
  # Raw distances
  distances_file <- file.path(output_dir, "bray_curtis_distances.csv")
  write.csv(distance_df, distances_file, row.names = FALSE)
  cat("Saved distances to:", distances_file, "\n")
  
  # Summary statistics per group
  summary_stats <- distance_df %>%
    group_by(Group) %>%
    summarise(
      N = n(),
      Mean = mean(BrayCurtis_Distance),
      SD = sd(BrayCurtis_Distance),
      Median = median(BrayCurtis_Distance),
      Min = min(BrayCurtis_Distance),
      Max = max(BrayCurtis_Distance)
    )
  stats_file <- file.path(output_dir, "bray_curtis_summary.csv")
  write.csv(summary_stats, stats_file, row.names = FALSE)
  cat("Saved summary statistics to:", stats_file, "\n")
  
  # Pairwise statistical tests
  if (length(unique(distance_df$Group)) >= 2) {
    test_results <- data.frame()
    groups <- unique(distance_df$Group)
    comparisons <- combn(groups, 2, simplify = FALSE)
    
    for (comp in comparisons) {
      group1 <- comp[1]
      group2 <- comp[2]
      
      data1 <- distance_df$BrayCurtis_Distance[distance_df$Group == group1]
      data2 <- distance_df$BrayCurtis_Distance[distance_df$Group == group2]
      
      if (length(data1) >= 2 && length(data2) >= 2) {
        test <- wilcox.test(data1, data2, exact = FALSE)
        
        test_results <- rbind(test_results, data.frame(
          Comparison = paste(group1, "vs", group2),
          N_Group1 = length(data1),
          N_Group2 = length(data2),
          Mean_Group1 = mean(data1),
          Mean_Group2 = mean(data2),
          P_value = test$p.value,
          P_value_rounded = round(test$p.value, 4),
          Significance = ifelse(test$p.value < 0.001, "***",
                               ifelse(test$p.value < 0.01, "**",
                                      ifelse(test$p.value < 0.05, "*", "ns")))
        ))
      }
    }
    
    if (nrow(test_results) > 0) {
      tests_file <- file.path(output_dir, "bray_curtis_statistics.csv")
      write.csv(test_results, tests_file, row.names = FALSE)
      cat("Saved statistical tests to:", tests_file, "\n")
    }
  }
}

# ----------------------------------------------------
# 8. Main analysis wrapper (updated)
# ----------------------------------------------------
analyze_within_sample_similarity <- function(ps_obj, 
                                             output_dir = "within_sample_analysis",
                                             show_p_values = FALSE,
                                             categorical_vars = NULL) {
  cat("=== WITHIN-SAMPLE SIMILARITY ANALYSIS ===\n")
  
  # Convert to relative abundance (optional but recommended)
  cat("\nConverting to relative abundance...\n")
  ps_rel <- transform_sample_counts(ps_obj, function(x) x / sum(x))
  
  # Calculate distances
  cat("\nCalculating Bray-Curtis distances...\n")
  distances <- calculate_within_sample_bray_curtis_simple(ps_rel)
  
  if (nrow(distances) == 0) {
    cat("\nNo valid distances calculated. Analysis stopped.\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Create main plot (overall, not stratified)
  cat("\nCreating overall visualization...\n")
  plot_bray_curtis_by_group(distances, output_dir, show_p_values = show_p_values)
  
  # Save results (distances, summary, stats)
  cat("\nSaving results...\n")
  save_bray_curtis_results(distances, output_dir)
  
  # If categorical variables are provided, generate stratified plots
  if (!is.null(categorical_vars) && length(categorical_vars) > 0) {
    cat("\n=== GENERATING STRATIFIED PLOTS ===\n")
    
    # Get patient-level metadata
    patient_meta <- get_patient_metadata(ps_obj)
    
    # For each categorical variable, create plots
    for (cat_var in categorical_vars) {
      plot_bray_curtis_by_categorical(distances, patient_meta, cat_var, 
                                      output_dir, show_p_values)
    }
  }
  
  # Print summary to console
  cat("\n=== ANALYSIS SUMMARY ===\n")
  cat("Total patients analyzed:", nrow(distances), "\n")
  cat("\nSummary by group:\n")
  print(distances %>%
          group_by(Group) %>%
          summarise(
            N = n(),
            Mean_Distance = round(mean(BrayCurtis_Distance), 4),
            SD = round(sd(BrayCurtis_Distance), 4),
            Median = round(median(BrayCurtis_Distance), 4)
          ))
  
  cat("\n=== COMPLETE ===\n")
  cat("Results saved in:", output_dir, "\n")
  
  return(list(distances = distances))
}

# ----------------------------------------------------
# 9. Identify categorical variables from metadata
# ----------------------------------------------------
# Extract metadata as data frame
ps_meta <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps))

# Find categorical variables (factor or character)
categorical_vars <- names(ps_meta)[sapply(ps_meta, function(x) is.factor(x) || is.character(x))]

# Remove variables we don't want to stratify by
categorical_vars <- setdiff(categorical_vars, 
                            c('Age', 'Group', 'Height', 'No_Patient', 'Weight', 'Timepoint', 'SampleID'))

cat("Categorical variables for stratification:\n")
print(categorical_vars)

# ----------------------------------------------------
# 10. RUN THE ANALYSIS WITH STRATIFICATION
# ----------------------------------------------------
results <- analyze_within_sample_similarity(
  ps_obj = ps,
  output_dir = "/home/sonec_iv/bray_curtis_MAGs",
  show_p_values = FALSE,          # set to TRUE if you prefer p-values instead of stars
  categorical_vars = categorical_vars
)
```
